title: Applied AI Engineer, AI Surfaces
company_focus:
AI and ERP software solutions for U.S. state and local
governments
description:
OpenGov is the leader in AI and ERP solutions for local and
state governments in the U.S. More than 2,000 cities,
counties, state agencies, school districts, and special
districts rely on the OpenGov Public Service Platform to
operate efficiently, adapt to change, and strengthen the
public trust. Category-leading products include enterprise
asset management, procurement and contract management,
accounting and budgeting, billing and revenue management,
permitting and licensing, and transparency and open data.
These solutions come together in the OpenGov ERP, allowing
public sector organizations to focus on priorities and
deliver maximum ROI with every dollar and decision in sync.
Learn about OpenGov’s mission to power more effective and
accountable government and the vision of high-performance
government for every community at OpenGov.com.

Job Summary

OpenGov is seeking an Applied AI Engineer, AI Surfaces to
own the API and middleware layer that connects OpenGov’s AI
capabilities to the applications and workflows end users
depend on. You will be among the first members of our Pune
AI engineering team, with real ownership over how AI
features reach production surfaces across the organization.

This is a software engineering role first. You bring strong
backend and API fundamentals, including clean service
design, production reliability, and the full lifecycle of a
deployed application, and apply them to the integration
challenges that sit between our AI platform and our internal
tooling/applications. System integration experience is a
genuine plus, but not a prerequisite. What matters is that
you build things that work reliably for real users, and that
you are motivated to grow your AI engineering skills in a
fast-moving environment.

You will work closely with the Sr. AI Platform Engineer to
surface AI capabilities through scalable, observable, and
maintainable interfaces, and collaborate with product and
frontend teams to deliver AI-powered experiences that are
fast, trustworthy, and built to last.

Core Responsibilities

- Design, build, and maintain the REST APIs, MCP Servers, and
  middleware services that expose AI capabilities (including
  but not limited to RAG pipelines, agent workflows, and
  data services) to OpenGov’s end-user applications.
- Build and maintain backend services using Python (FastAPI,
  Flask) that support AI-driven workflows and enterprise
  integrations.
- Own reliability, performance, and observability of AI-
  powered services: logging, latency monitoring, error
  handling, and graceful degradation under load.
- Integrate AI features into OpenGov’s existing application
  surfaces, working with well-defined interfaces from the AI
  platform team rather than rebuilding the model layer.
- Collaborate with product and frontend teams to deliver AI-
  native user experiences that meet production standards for
  usability, performance, and security.
- Implement authentication, authorization, and secure access
  patterns across internal AI tools and APIs.
- Support integration of AI workflows into enterprise
  platforms such as Slack, Salesforce, and other SaaS
  systems.
- Containerize and deploy services using Docker and cloud-
  native infrastructure; contribute to CI/CD pipeline
  development and maintenance.
- Support rapid prototyping of AI-powered features while
  maintaining a clear and disciplined path from prototype to
  production-grade deployment.
- Actively develop AI engineering knowledge on the job. you
  will grow into an AI integration specialist, not just an
  API application engineer.

Required Experience

- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a
  related field.
- 3–6 years of experience in software engineering, backend
  development, or platform engineering roles.
- Strong Python skills with hands-on experience building and
  maintaining production REST APIs using FastAPI, Flask, or
  equivalent frameworks.
- Familiarity with Model Context Protocol (MCP) and experience
  building and/or consuming MCP-compatible tool servers.
- Proven ability to design, deploy, and operate production
  application services, not just internal prototypes or
  demos.
- Solid understanding of API design patterns, versioning,
  authentication and authorization, and service reliability
  fundamentals (retries, timeouts, circuit breakers).
- Comfortable with containerized deployment (Docker) and/or
  Snowflake SPCS.
- Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines and Git-
  based release workflows.
- Strong debugging and troubleshooting skills across backend
  and infrastructure layers.
- Collaborative by default: works well with product, frontend,
  and data teams without creating bottlenecks.
- Growth orientation: genuinely motivated to develop AI
  engineering skills on the job.

Nice to Have

- Experience integrating LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini,
  etc.) into production application services.
- Familiarity with LangChain, Snowflake Cortex or similar AI
  application frameworks.
- Experience building AI-powered internal tools, copilots, or
  enterprise dashboards.
- Experience integrating with enterprise platforms such as
  Slack, Salesforce, Workday, NetSuite, or similar SaaS
  systems.
- Familiarity with Snowflake data architecture or Snowflake-
  hosted APIs.
- Understanding of async patterns and streaming API responses
  (SSE, WebSockets) relevant to AI-powered UX.
- Experience collaborating with cross-functional, non-
  technical stakeholders.

Why OpenGov?

A Mission That Matters.

At OpenGov, public service is personal. We are passionate
about our mission to power more effective and accountable
government. Government that operates efficiently, adapts to
change, and strengthens public trust.  Some people say this
is boring.  We think it’s the core of our democracy.

Opportunity to Innovate

The next great wave of innovation is unfolding with AI, and
it will impact everything—from the way we work to the way
governments interact with their residents. Join a trusted
team with the passion, technology, and expertise to drive
innovation and bring AI to local government. We’ve touched
2,000 communities so far, and we’re just getting started.

A Team of Passionate, Driven People

This isn’t your typical 9-to-5 job; we operate in a fast-
paced, results-driven environment where impact matters more
than simply clocking in and out. Our global team of 800+
employees is united in our commitment to challenge the
status quo. OpenGov is headquartered in San Francisco and
has offices in Atlanta, Boston, Buenos Aires, Chicago,
Dubuque, Plano, and Pune.

A Place to Make Your Mark

We pride ourselves on our performance-based culture, where
every employee is encouraged to jump in head-first and take
action to help us improve. If you have a great idea, we want
to hear it. Excellent performance is recognized and
rewarded, and we love to promote from within.

Benefits That Work for You

Enjoy an award-winning workplace with the benefits to match,
including:

- Comprehensive healthcare options for individuals and
  families
- Flexible vacation policy and paid company holidays
- 401(k) with company match
- Paid parental leave, wellness stipends, and HSA
  contributions
- Professional development and growth opportunities
- A collaborative office environment with weekly catered
  lunches.
responsibilities: Design, build, and maintain REST APIs, MCP Servers, and middleware services that expose AI capabilities to end-user applications
Build and maintain backend services using Python (FastAPI, Flask) supporting AI-driven workflows and enterprise integrations
Own reliability, performance, and observability of AI-powered services including logging, latency monitoring, and error handling
Integrate AI features into existing application surfaces using well-defined interfaces from the AI platform team
Collaborate with product and frontend teams to deliver AI-native user experiences meeting production standards
Implement authentication, authorization, and secure access patterns across internal AI tools and APIs
Support integration of AI workflows into enterprise platforms such as Slack, Salesforce, and other SaaS systems

use_cases: RAG pipelines for government data access and retrieval
Agent-based workflows for public sector process automation
AI-powered enterprise integrations with Slack, Salesforce, and other SaaS platforms
AI-driven features in permitting, licensing, budgeting, and asset management applications
Internal AI-powered tools and copilots for government operations
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is explicitly described as "a software engineering role
first." The engineer builds APIs and middleware that connect
AI capabilities built by others (the AI platform team) to
end-user applications. They work with well-defined
interfaces from the AI platform team "rather than rebuilding
the model layer," placing them in a supporting role that
surfaces and integrates AI—not building, training, or fine-
tuning models.
